ddls:
CREATE TABLE "students" (
  "student_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each student */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the student */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the student */,
  "grade" INTEGER /* Grade level of the student */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the student */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the student */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the student */,
  "address" TEXT /* Residential address of the student */,
  "is_car_owner" INTEGER /* Whether the student owns a car (1 = yes, 0 = no) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("student_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "professional_skills" (
  "skill_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each professional skill */,
  "student_id" INTEGER /* ID of the student who took the professional skill assessment */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the professional skill was assessed */,
  "score" INTEGER /* Score achieved by the student in the professional skill assessment */,
  "skill_name" TEXT /* Name of the professional skill */,
  "skill_category" TEXT /* Category of the professional skill (e.g., programming, communication) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("skill_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_professional_skills_student_id FOREIGN KEY ("student_id") REFERENCES students ("student_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "speech_skills" (
  "skill_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each speech skill */,
  "student_id" INTEGER /* ID of the student who took the speech skill assessment */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the speech skill was assessed */,
  "score" INTEGER /* Score achieved by the student in the speech skill assessment */,
  "skill_name" TEXT /* Name of the speech skill */,
  "skill_category" TEXT /* Category of the speech skill (e.g., presentation, public speaking) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("skill_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_speech_skills_student_id FOREIGN KEY ("student_id") REFERENCES students ("student_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "grades" (
  "grade_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each grade */,
  "student_id" INTEGER /* ID of the student who earned the grade */,
  "grade" INTEGER /* Grade earned by the student */,
  "grade_category" TEXT /* Category of the grade (e.g., A, B, C) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("grade_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_grades_student_id FOREIGN KEY ("student_id") REFERENCES students ("student_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "grade_levels" (
  "level_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each grade level */,
  "level_name" TEXT /* Name of the grade level */,
  "min_grade" INTEGER /* Minimum grade for the grade level */,
  "max_grade" INTEGER /* Maximum grade for the grade level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("level_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "professional_skills_levels" (
  "level_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each professional skills level */,
  "level_name" TEXT /* Name of the professional skills level */,
  "min_score" INTEGER /* Minimum score for the professional skills level */,
  "max_score" INTEGER /* Maximum score for the professional skills level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("level_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "speech_skills_levels" (
  "level_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each speech skills level */,
  "level_name" TEXT /* Name of the speech skills level */,
  "min_score" INTEGER /* Minimum score for the speech skills level */,
  "max_score" INTEGER /* Maximum score for the speech skills level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("level_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., educator, administrator, researcher) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "courses" (
  "course_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each course */,
  "course_name" TEXT /* Name of the course */,
  "course_description" TEXT /* Description of the course */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("course_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "student_courses" (
  "student_id" INTEGER /* ID of the student */,
  "course_id" INTEGER /* ID of the course */,
  "grade" INTEGER /* Grade earned by the student in the course */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("student_id", "course_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "professional_skills_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each professional skills category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the professional skills category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "speech_skills_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each speech skills category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the speech skills category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)
